task_completion.description: Verify that the 'auth_summary' index has been created in Splunk.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk 'Indexes' settings page (Settings > Indexes) showing 'auth_summary' in the list of indexes.

task_completion.description: Verify that a saved search named 'Auth_Metrics_Collector' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Searches, reports, and alerts' page or a saved search configuration screen showing the name 'Auth_Metrics_Collector'.

task_completion.description: Verify the search query aggregates from 'security_logs' and uses the 'collect' command to route to 'auth_summary'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the search string in the Splunk search bar or saved search edit window. It must contain 'index="security_logs"' (or similar), an aggregation command (like stats, timechart, or sistats), and end with '| collect index="auth_summary"' (or similar syntax).

task_completion.description: Verify the saved search is scheduled to run every 4 hours.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the schedule settings of the 'Auth_Metrics_Collector' saved search. The cron schedule should be explicitly set to '0 */4 * * *' or the UI should indicate it runs 'Every 4 hours'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'auth_summary' index contains at least one pre-computed summary event.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Splunk search execution screen where the query is 'index="auth_summary"' (or similar) and the results table/list shows at least one event returned, indicating the collect command successfully populated the index.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Splunk Web UI rather than just editing configuration files via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Splunk Enterprise web interface (typically with the Splunk logo, navigation bar, and dark/light theme UI elements) loaded in a web browser at localhost:8000.

integrity.description: Verify that the summary events are genuine Splunk search results and not manually mocked data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The events shown in the 'auth_summary' index should have standard Splunk fields (like _time, host, source, sourcetype) and formatting consistent with the Splunk Search & Reporting app.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the Splunk UI by just using curl commands or script injections to fake the task completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show the agent navigating through the Splunk UI menus (Settings > Indexes, Search, Save As > Report/Alert) rather than exclusively operating in a terminal window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target index name: 'auth_summary'. Saved search name: 'Auth_Metrics_Collector'. Source index: 'security_logs'. Cron schedule: '0 */4 * * *'. The search query must contain 'index=security_logs' (or similar) and '| collect index=auth_summary'.
